name: BOL-148

dosing: BOL-148 is a research-only compound not available to patients outside clinical trials.
The Hannover clinical trial used 0.03 mg/kg per dose, roughly 3 mg per person.
Despite being an LSD derivative, it is non-hallucinogenic at any tested dose.
preparations: Research-grade BOL-148 was synthesized by THC Pharm in Germany and is not available to individuals.
The compound is created by adding a bromine atom to LSD at the R2 position, eliminating psychedelic effects.
schedule: The Hannover trial protocol used 3 doses over 15 days, followed by a booster at 6 months.
All administration was under clinical supervision; no self-dosing protocols exist.
A proposed Phase II trial across 10 centers in Germany and Austria was never completed.

sideEffects: No significant side effects reported in clinical trials
Cannot address PTSD as the psychedelic experience component is absent
Less effective for migraine than LSD or methysergide
